perturbation.original_text: 1.6 "Research Plan" means the research plan attached hereto as Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 "Research Plan" means a flexible document that outlines the initial research goals but is subject to unilateral modification by Anixa at any time without prior notice or consent from OntoChem.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the definition of 'Research Plan'. Originally, it was a fixed document (Exhibit A). The modified definition allows Anixa to unilaterally change the Research Plan, potentially altering OntoChem's obligations without their consent. This contradicts Section 2.1, which implies a mutually agreed-upon Research Plan, creating uncertainty about the scope of OntoChem's required activities and Anixa's ability to change the agreement’s core parameters without negotiation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Performance. The Parties will diligently perform their respective activities set forth in the Research Plan (such activities, collectively, the "Research Program") in accordance with the timelines set forth therein, with the objective of identifying Hit Compounds and Lead Scaffolds that modulate the applicable Target. Without limiting the foregoing, OntoChem will (a) provide all deliverables set forth in the Research Plan (each, a "Deliverable") and (b) obtain any authorizations, approvals and licenses required for performance of the Research Plan. If any terms set forth in the Research Plan conflict with the terms set forth in this Agreement, the terms of this Agreement will control unless expressly indicated to the contrary in the Research Plan. The Research Plan may not be amended without the prior written consent of both Parties. If, from time to time, the Parties desire to expand the scope of the Research Program, then they will negotiate in good faith a potential amendment of the Research Plan in regard to such expanded scope, on commercially reasonable terms, but neither Party will be obligated to enter into any such amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Research Plan" to allow Anixa to unilaterally modify it. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which states, "The Research Plan may not be amended without the prior written consent of both Parties." The changed definition removes the requirement for mutual consent, creating a clear contradiction about how the Research Plan can be modified, thus impacting OntoChem's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 Inventions Owned by OntoChem. OntoChem will own, and Anixa hereby assigns to OntoChem, all right, title and interest in and to all Inventions directed to (a) any methods of generating or screening compound libraries and (b) the Rejected Hit Compounds (including the composition, use or manufacture thereof), in the case of this clause (b), effective as of the Selection Deadline (collectively (clauses (a) and (b)), "OntoChem Inventions"). As between the Parties, OntoChem will exclusively control the filing, prosecution, maintenance and enforcement of any patents and patent applications claiming OntoChem Inventions.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 Inventions Owned by OntoChem. OntoChem will own, and Anixa hereby assigns to OntoChem, all right, title and interest in and to all Inventions directed to (a) any methods of generating or screening compound libraries and (b) the Rejected Hit Compounds (including the composition, use or manufacture thereof), in the case of this clause (b), effective as of the Selection Deadline (collectively (clauses (a) and (b)), "OntoChem Inventions"), unless Anixa, at its sole discretion, determines that such Inventions are critical to their commercial strategy. As between the Parties, OntoChem will exclusively control the filing, prosecution, maintenance and enforcement of any patents and patent applications claiming OntoChem Inventions, but Anixa retains the right to seize control of such filings if it deems necessary for market dominance.
perturbation.explanation: The added clauses introduce uncertainty about OntoChem's ownership of 'OntoChem Inventions'. While the section initially assigns these inventions to OntoChem, the added phrase 'unless Anixa, at its sole discretion, determines that such Inventions are critical to their commercial strategy' and 'but Anixa retains the right to seize control of such filings if it deems necessary for market dominance.' allows Anixa to claim ownership and control of these inventions. This directly contradicts the original statement that OntoChem will own and control these inventions, particularly undermining OntoChem's rights related to Rejected Hit Compounds and methods of generating compound libraries. This is because the modified text causes In-Text Contradiction on whether OntoChem exclusively controls filing of patensts of rejected hit compounds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Inventions Owned by OntoChem. OntoChem will own, and Anixa hereby assigns to OntoChem, all right, title and interest in and to all Inventions directed to (a) any methods of generating or screening compound libraries and (b) the Rejected Hit Compounds (including the composition, use or manufacture thereof), in the case of this clause (b), effective as of the Selection Deadline (collectively (clauses (a) and (b)), "OntoChem Inventions"), unless Anixa, at its sole discretion, determines that such Inventions are critical to their commercial strategy. As between the Parties, OntoChem will exclusively control the filing, prosecution, maintenance and enforcement of any patents and patent applications claiming OntoChem Inventions, but Anixa retains the right to seize control of such filings if it deems necessary for market dominance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text in 6.2 assigns ownership and control of 'OntoChem Inventions' exclusively to OntoChem. However, the added clauses in the changed text give Anixa the right to claim ownership and seize control of patents related to these inventions under certain circumstances ('unless Anixa, at its sole discretion, determines that such Inventions are critical to their commercial strategy' and 'Anixa retains the right to seize control of such filings if it deems necessary for market dominance'). This directly conflicts with the initial assignment of exclusive ownership and control to OntoChem, creating a structural flaw and in-text contradiction as defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Termination by Anixa. This Agreement may be terminated by Anixa, without cause, upon at least thirty (30) days written notice to OntoChem.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Termination by Anixa. This Agreement may be terminated by Anixa, without cause, upon at least thirty (30) days written notice to OntoChem, unless such termination occurs during a period when Anixa deems the Research Program to be of high strategic value, in which case termination requires Anixa to compensate OntoChem an amount determined solely by Anixa but not less than one Euro. In cases where Research Program shows poor performance, Anixa can also terminate the agreement immediately without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause provides Anixa with an undefined 'high strategic value' exception to the standard termination notice period, and even allow them to terminate the agreement immedately with no prior notice. Further, it allows Anixa to dictate the compensation to be paid to OntoChem in this instance, provided the amount is not lower than a nominal amount of one Euro. The final addition enables Anixa to circumvent the notice period in cases of 'poor performance' without clear metrics. This contradicts the original thirty (30) days written notice clause. This creates an In-Text Contradiction related to the thirty (30) days of termination, and when a termination can be made, creating uncertainty in the initial termination obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Termination by Anixa. This Agreement may be terminated by Anixa, without cause, upon at least thirty (30) days written notice to OntoChem, unless such termination occurs during a period when Anixa deems the Research Program to be of high strategic value, in which case termination requires Anixa to compensate OntoChem an amount determined solely by Anixa but not less than one Euro. In cases where Research Program shows poor performance, Anixa can also terminate the agreement immediately without prior notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds conditions under which the agreement can be terminated immediately or with less than thirty days' notice, and with compensation determined solely by Anixa (but no less than one Euro), directly contradicting the original clause's requirement of at least thirty (30) days' written notice for termination without cause. This introduces a clear and direct conflict, making the termination terms inconsistent and creating uncertainty about the obligations of each party. The contradicted text is the original agreement, which is now made uncertain due to the changed text.
